problem.statement: Partner onboarding, first-store readiness, approval, publication, and payout setup must form one governed journey instead of disconnected surface-specific records and manual checks.
problem.currentWorkaround: Operators manually reconcile partner, store, document, visit, publication, and payout state across separate screens and services.

actors.role: Captures and maintains an owned partner and first-store onboarding draft with evidence.
Reads the governed activation, readiness, store scope, and team state for the partner context.
Reviews documents and evidence, links unowned stores, and applies governed activation and publication decisions.
Discovers a store only after all publication gates are satisfied.

surfaces.exclusionReason: Captain assignment and fulfillment begin in later order and dispatch journeys after store publication.

outcome.statement: A partner can be onboarded from field draft to a client-visible first store with one traceable state model, while WLT exclusively owns raw payout details.
outcome.primaryMetric: percentage of submitted partner drafts reaching a governed terminal decision without manual cross-system reconciliation
guardrailMetrics: zero stores reassigned between partners outside an explicit future transfer journey
zero raw payout account identifiers returned by DSH
zero client-visible stores failing any publication gate
zero duplicate transition audit events for identical retries
outcome.baselineState: Existing implementation contains disconnected readiness checks, weak retry identity, and legacy raw payout fields in DSH.
outcome.targetState: Every mutation is authorization-scoped, concurrency-safe, idempotent, audited, and readable on all required surfaces.
outcome.observationWindow: one complete onboarding cycle plus seven days of post-publication monitoring

criteria: Field agents can create, resume, save, and submit only their own partner draft.
Submission is blocked until legal identity, first-store profile, and a WLT payout destination reference are complete.
All uploaded documents must be independently approved before operations review.
Partner activation requires a submitted evidence-bearing field visit linked to the partner store.
Client publication requires active, visible, serviceable, partner-ready, catalog-approved, and marketing-visible store state.
A store already owned by one partner cannot be linked to another partner.
Identical transition and payout retries replay the original result; payload changes under the same key are rejected.
DSH persists and returns only WLT payout references and masked compatibility values.
Partner and control-panel surfaces read back the committed activation and readiness state.
failureStates: raw payout data stored or returned by DSH
surface reports success before committed readback

business: One partner may own multiple stores, but one store has at most one onboarding owner.
Control-panel approval is distinct from field evidence capture.
WLT is the sole owner of raw payout destination data.
Client visibility is a store publication outcome, not merely a partner status label.
Every material transition has actor, surface, correlation, retry identity, and immutable audit.
negative: A field agent cannot approve their own evidence.
A partner cannot self-publish a store.
A store cannot be reassigned by the generic link operation.
DSH cannot persist raw account number, IBAN, or payout mobile data after binding a WLT reference.
A stale version cannot mutate partner state.
A reused idempotency key cannot represent a different payload.
A hidden, unserviceable, unready, catalog-unapproved, or marketing-hidden store cannot appear to clients.

execution.appetite: Close JRN-001 sequentially across all mandatory FS-01 through FS-18 slices, then stop before JRN-002.
fixedConstraints: Use GitHub Remote sambassam as execution truth.
DSH owns operational partner and store state; WLT owns financial payout truth.
No store reassignment through the generic onboarding link route.
No raw payout identifiers persisted or returned by DSH.
No next journey begins before the JRN-001 decision is recorded.
variableScope: non-breaking compatibility mappings for masked payout fields
forbiddenScope: pull request, merge, release, or production deployment
fabricated runtime, QA, security, finance, or visual evidence
unknowns: independent runtime and visual evidence remain unavailable until CI and surface runs execute on the final commit
historical raw payout rows without a WLT reference require a separately verified migration run before production cleanup
